title: Stellar Wave 0 — Chain adapter boundary (enabler, no Stellar logic)
goal: Introduce a neutral ChainAdapter abstraction and a ChainId type, then re-express the existing Solana code behind that adapter without changing any Solana behavior. Define the chain-neutral SemanticFacts/AccountSignerState/ChainAuditMetadata types the brain consumes, add a chainRegistry, implement SolanaChainAdapter, replace the hardcoded chain: "solana" literal types with ChainId, and inject chain/network from config in the hosted evaluation service. No Stellar implementation in this wave.

context.analysisSource: Today there is no chain abstraction. The signing interface is typed directly on Solana's VersionedTransaction (back/services/support/signer/signerAdapterContracts.ts). The chain is hardcoded as a TypeScript literal chain: "solana" in shared/types/executionGatewayContracts.ts (ActionCandidate at line 39/79, AuditEvent at line 51) and as string literals in hosted/evaluate/evaluationService.ts (around lines 143, 180-181, 201) and in the transfer/swap gateways. The LLM router prompt also hardcodes "Solana" (hosted/llm/llmRouterPrompt.ts line 9). Without a seam, any Stellar code would re-leak coupling into the brain.

openQuestions.question: Should ChainAdapter.cosign/submit be optional methods on a single ChainAdapter, or split into a separate SignerAdapter-per-chain abstraction?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Keep cosign/submit OPTIONAL on a single ChainAdapter in Wave 0. The brain only requires decode + buildAuditMetadata to evaluate an action; signing/submission is an edge concern that not every chain exercises the same way. A single adapter with optional signing methods keeps the seam minimal and avoids introducing a second registry before any chain needs it. If a later chain's multisig/co-signing flow grows complex, splitting out a per-chain SignerAdapter is a deferred, additive change that does not break this contract.

openQuestions.question: Where exactly should chain/network be injected — the request payload or server config?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Inject from SERVER CONFIG in Wave 0, not the request payload. The default config resolves to Solana, preserving today's behavior with zero runtime change. Trusting a client-supplied chain in the request payload would let a caller influence which adapter (and which policy thresholds) applies, which is a guardrail-bypass risk. Server-config injection keeps the chain identity an operator decision. Surfacing chain selection through request payloads or MCP inputs is deferred to a later Stellar wave and would require its own validation.

acceptanceCriteria: ChainAdapter, ChainId, and SemanticFacts are exported from a single contracts file (shared/types/chainContracts.ts), alongside AccountSignerState and ChainAuditMetadata.
SemanticFacts carries the chain-neutral fields the policy engine already consumes: actionKind, sourceAddress, recipientAddress, asset, amount, amountUsd, plus optional risk flags.
Solana is re-expressed behind a SolanaChainAdapter with NO behavior change; existing Solana tests stay green.
chainRegistry resolves a ChainAdapter by ChainId and fails clearly (CHAIN_ADAPTER_NOT_REGISTERED) for unregistered chains, never silently defaulting to Solana.
The chain: "solana" literal types in shared/types/executionGatewayContracts.ts are replaced by ChainId.
hosted/evaluate/evaluationService.ts injects chain/network from configuration instead of hardcoding "solana".
No Stellar logic is added in this wave.

Brain files are untouched: hosted/policy/policyEngine.ts, hosted/llm/llmDecisionAdapter.ts, hosted/llm/llmDecisionSanitizer.ts, the COMPASS_DECISIONS contract semantics in shared/types/executionGatewayContracts.ts, and back/services/mcp/proxy/*.
nonGoals: No Stellar implementation, no Stellar SDK, no Soroban/Horizon code.
No change to Solana runtime behavior — Solana must keep working unchanged in parallel.
No change to the brain: policy engine, LLM judge, decision sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS semantics, and MCP proxy.
No rewording of the LLM router prompt beyond what the seam requires (deferred).
No new MCP tools and no signer behavior change.
No re-typing of signerAdapterContracts.ts off VersionedTransaction in this wave.
verification: Manual: resolveChainAdapter("solana") returns an adapter with chainId === "solana"
Manual: confirm decoded SemanticFacts for a representative Solana transfer/swap match the current Solana decode output (parity)

tasks.title: Add RED tests for chain contracts and registry
tasks.description: Write tests, before implementation, asserting chainContracts.ts exports ChainId, SemanticFacts, AccountSignerState, ChainAuditMetadata, and ChainAdapter; that a class with only required members (chainId, decode, buildAuditMetadata) compiles; that resolveChainAdapter resolves Solana and rejects unregistered chains; and that no new chain file imports legacy/.
testsSpec: chainContracts exports ChainAdapter, ChainId, SemanticFacts, AccountSignerState, ChainAuditMetadata (compile/shape)
A class implementing only chainId + decode + buildAuditMetadata satisfies ChainAdapter (optional methods are optional)
resolveChainAdapter("solana") returns { ok: true, adapter } with adapter.chainId === "solana"
No legacy/ import appears in any new chain file

tasks.title: Implement chain contracts and registry
tasks.description: Create shared/types/chainContracts.ts (ChainId, SemanticFacts, AccountSignerState, ChainAuditMetadata, ChainAdapter) and back/services/chain/chainRegistry.ts (resolveChainAdapter by ChainId, Solana registered, CHAIN_ADAPTER_NOT_REGISTERED otherwise). Keep types separate from registry behavior.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for SolanaChainAdapter parity
tasks.description: Write tests, before implementation, asserting SolanaChainAdapter.decode produces SemanticFacts equal to the current Solana decode output for a representative transfer and swap payload, and that buildAuditMetadata contains no raw tx bytes or secret material.
testsSpec: SolanaChainAdapter.decode(transferPayload) yields SemanticFacts matching the current Solana transfer decode
SolanaChainAdapter.decode(swapPayload) yields SemanticFacts matching the current Solana swap decode
buildAuditMetadata output contains no raw transaction bytes, private keys, secret material, or raw prompts
cosign/submit, when present, deserialize the opaque string payload at the edge without changing Solana signing behavior

tasks.title: Implement SolanaChainAdapter wrapping existing Solana modules
tasks.description: Create back/services/chain/solana/solanaChainAdapter.ts that delegates to the existing Solana tx-build/sign/connection modules and adapts their inputs/outputs to the neutral types. Must not re-implement or alter Solana logic. Register it in chainRegistry.

tasks.title: Replace chain literal types with ChainId and inject chain/network from config
tasks.description: Edit shared/types/executionGatewayContracts.ts to change the chain: "solana" literal types (ActionCandidate, AuditEvent) to chain: ChainId, preserving the COMPASS_DECISIONS contract semantics and runtime value "solana". Edit hosted/evaluate/evaluationService.ts to read chain/network from server config instead of the literal "solana" strings around lines 143, 180-181, 201; default config resolves to Solana. Do NOT touch the brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, MCP proxy).

tasks.title: Validate full suite and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite to confirm all pre-existing Solana tests stay green, chain is typed as ChainId across contracts and evaluation service, and no brain files or legacy/ imports changed. Stop for parent review before any Stellar wave begins.

implementationEvidence.mode: Adapter seam, additive; Solana behavior preserved
filesEdited: shared/types/executionGatewayContracts.ts (chain: "solana" -> ChainId on ActionCandidateInput, ActionCandidate, AuditEvent)
hosted/evaluate/evaluationService.ts (chain/network injected via resolveChainConfig instead of literal "solana" at the 3 sites)
commands: npm run test:back -> 320 passed (31 files), incl. 8 new chain tests
tsc --noEmit -> only 1 PRE-EXISTING error remains (back/services/__tests__/mcpProxyDispatcher.test.ts stale import '../mcp/mcpProxyContracts'); zero new errors from Wave 0
npm run lint (whole repo) -> 5 PRE-EXISTING errors in hosted/evaluate/evaluationValidators.ts (unused imports), unrelated to Wave 0
implementationEvidence.preExistingBaselineNote: Branch baseline already had 1 tsc error and 5 lint errors before Wave 0 (verified via git stash). Wave 0 introduces zero new tsc/lint errors and leaves the full backend suite green.
deviations: SolanaChainAdapter.decode implements the SOL System-transfer payload (the concrete serialized VersionedTransaction the current builder produces); swap/other-program decode is deferred since the live Solana flow derives policy context from tool arguments, not from on-chain tx decode.
amountUsd is 0 at decode time by design: structural decode stays offline/deterministic and USD valuation is layered by the gateway/price provider.
Brain untouched: policyEngine.ts, llmDecisionAdapter.ts, llmDecisionSanitizer.ts, COMPASS_DECISIONS semantics, and back/services/mcp/proxy/* were not modified.
Gateways (transferGateway.ts/swapGateway.ts) still pass the literal value "solana", which remains assignable to ChainId; no behavior change.
